details.OnetDescription: Provide healthcare services typically performed by a physician, under the supervision of a physician. Conduct complete physicals, provide treatment, and counsel patients. May, in some cases, prescribe medication. Must graduate from an accredited educational program for physician assistants.

Tasks.TaskDescription: Interpret diagnostic test results for deviations from normal.
Administer or order diagnostic tests, such as x-ray, electrocardiogram, and laboratory tests.
Prescribe therapy or medication with physician approval.
Perform therapeutic procedures, such as injections, immunizations, suturing and wound care, and infection management.
Visit and observe patients on hospital rounds or house calls, updating charts, ordering therapy, and reporting back to physician.
Prescribe therapy or medication with physician approval.
Perform therapeutic procedures, such as injections, immunizations, suturing and wound care, and infection management.
Order medical and laboratory supplies and equipment.
Instruct and counsel patients about prescribed therapeutic regimens, normal growth and development, family planning, emotional problems of daily living, and health maintenance.
Obtain, compile, and record patient medical data, including health history, progress notes, and results of physical examination.
Make tentative diagnoses and decisions about management and treatment of patients.
Obtain, compile, and record patient medical data, including health history, progress notes, and results of physical examination.
Supervise and coordinate activities of technicians and technical assistants.
Provide physicians with assistance during surgery or complicated medical procedures.
Examine patients to obtain information about their physical condition.
Visit and observe patients on hospital rounds or house calls, updating charts, ordering therapy, and reporting back to physician.
Visit and observe patients on hospital rounds or house calls, updating charts, ordering therapy, and reporting back to physician.

Skills.ElementDescription: Understanding people's reactions.
Figuring out how to use new ideas or things.
Using scientific rules and strategies to solve problems.
Thinking about the pros and cons of different ways to solve a problem.
Deciding what kind of tools and equipment are needed to do a job.
Watching gauges, dials, or display screens to make sure a machine is working.
Using the best training or teaching strategies for learning new things.
Using equipment or systems.
Planning and doing the basic maintenance on equipment.
Repairing machines or systems using the right tools.
Testing how well a product or service works.
Managing your time and the time of other people.
Making spending decisions and keeping track of what is spent.
Writing computer programs.
Talking people into changing their minds or their behavior.
Using math to solve problems.
Reading work-related information.
Listening to others, not interrupting, and asking good questions.
Figuring out what a product or service needs to be able to do.
Selecting and managing the best workers for a job.
Bringing people together to solve differences.
Installing equipment, machines, wiring, or computer programs.
Changing what is done based on other people's actions.
Talking to others.
Teaching people how to do something.
Keeping track of how well people and/or groups are doing in order to make improvements.
Writing things for co-workers or customers.
Making equipment and technology useful for customers.
Noticing a problem and figuring out the best way to solve it.
Figuring out what is causing equipment, machines, wiring, or computer programs to not work.
Thinking about the pros and cons of different options and picking the best one.
Figuring out how a system should work and how changes in the future will affect it.
Measuring how well a system is working and how to improve it.
Managing equipment and materials.
Looking for ways to help people.

WorkEnvironment.WorkEnvironment: Provide healthcare services typically performed by a physician, under the supervision of a physician. Conduct complete physicals, provide treatment, and counsel patients. May, in some cases, prescribe medication. Must graduate from an accredited educational program for physician assistants.

InterestDataList.ElementDescription: Helping, teaching, advising, assisting, or providing service to others.
Studying and researching scientific subjects and human behavior.
Following procedures and regulations to organize information or data, typically in a business setting.
